summary: Postinstall fetches mutable nightly binary and auto-exposes installer machine via public Cloudflare tunnel
details: scripts/postinstall.js downloads a platform-specific `companion-*` binary from `https://github.com/palmthree-studio/whiteboard-agent/releases/download/nightly/...` — a mutable, floating `nightly` tag rather than a tag pinned to the package version 1.4.23 — chmods it 0755, and executes it. There is no hash or signature verification, so whatever bytes sit behind the `nightly` tag at install time are run on the installer's machine; future republishes (intentional or via account compromise) silently ship new code to every prior installer on their next `npm install`. On non-TTY installs (the typical CI shape), the postinstall additionally generates default `admin` credentials with a random-hex password, writes them to `~/.whiteboard-agent/config.json`, and detaches `wendy start` as a daemon — which launches the opaque companion binary on 127.0.0.1:3001 and runs `cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:3001` to assign a public `*.trycloudflare.com` URL. Within seconds of `npm install` completing on a CI runner, the build host is serving an HTTP daemon reachable from the public internet, behind credentials that may be guessable or recoverable from the on-disk config. Installer never consented to either the unpinned binary execution or the inbound exposure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
